text: With anthropogenic climate change on the increase and posing significant threats to the environment, it has become imperative to investigate and mitigate the health impacts associated with climate change and also assess the environmental impact of the health sector. Medical imaging is known to generate huge amounts of clinical carbon emissions. This article seeks to highlight the environmental challenges in the imaging sector, demonstrate some environmentally friendly alternatives and show ways in which radiologists can be at the forefront of reducing health care's carbon footprint and implement environmentally friendly practices within their departments.
